summary: Deterministic proof that adapter human review prompts use failure-oriented human review lenses and that Cautilus carries those lenses from adapter or review packet input into rendered prompts.

evidenceScope.targetSurface: adapter human review prompt shape contract
evidenceScope.claimBoundary: Only the adapter-contract claim that human review prompts should point at human-visible failure is marked satisfied.
notClaimed: Cautilus semantically validates arbitrary consumer-written natural-language review prompts.
Every external consumer adapter has a failure-oriented human review prompt.
A live reviewer agreed with a specific rendered prompt.

protectedBehaviors: review prompt input preserves humanReviewPrompts[0].id as real-user
rendered review prompt includes the Human Review Lenses section
rendered review prompt includes the failure-oriented lens Where would a real user still judge the candidate worse despite benchmark wins
output-under-test review mode keeps realized behavior evidence visible next to the rendered review prompt
build-review-packet collects adapter human_review_prompts into cautilus.review_packet.v1
the adapter fixture prompt asks where the workflow is still brittle
BuildReviewPacket collects human_review_prompts into the review packet
the runtime fixture prompt asks where the workflow is still misleading
CLI build-prompt-input carries humanReviewPrompts forward from the review packet
CLI render-prompt emits current evidence, output-under-test context, and review prompt material together for human judgment

decision.reason: The checked-in adapter contract names failure-oriented human review prompt shape; review packet, prompt-input, renderer, runtime, and CLI tests verify that those human review lenses are collected and rendered next to current evidence and output-under-test context instead of being dropped or hidden.
